data.title: Pyramid Stacker
data.solution:
I=input
N,H=map(int,I().split())
C=I();L=[]
for i in range(H,0,-1):s=C[:i*i];j=len(s)%i;L=[(" "*(H-i)+" ".join((s[-i:],s[-j:]+s[-i:-j])[j>0])).rstrip()]+L;C=C[i*i:]
print("\n".join(L))
data.statement:
There are [[N]] toy cubes of equal size, each labelled with some uppercase English letter ({{A}}-{{Z}}). Your job is to build a 3D pyramid using these cubes. The pyramid should have [[H]] layers; the layers are labelled 1 to [[H]] from <<top to bottom>>. However, you are told that you may not have enough toy cubes to construct all [[H]] layers.

From bottom to top, build the layer labelled [[i]] as follows:
• Mark out an [[i]]x[[i]] empty grid centred on top of the previously-built layers. The 3D pyramid is viewed such that <<the bottom-most row of the grid is the front-most row in the pyramid>>.
• Take the next toy cube (from the given order of cubes) and place it in the grid. The grid is filled row by row from <<top to bottom>>, and each row is filled from <<left to right>>.

The 3D pyramid will be fully built once all toy cubes are placed. How will the 3D pyramid look like <<from the front>> once it's been fully built?

Examples:
[[H]] = 3, Toy cubes = ABCDEFGHIJKLMN ([[N]] = 14)
```============ Top View ============   ======== Front View ========
+---+---+---+                                 +---+
| A | B | C |   +---+---+                     | N |       Layer 1
+---+---+---+   | J | K |   +---+           +-+-+-+-+
| D | E | F |   +---+---+   | N |           | L | M |     Layer 2
+---+---+---+   | L | M |   +---+         +-+-+-+-+-+-+
| G | H | I |   +---+---+                 | G | H | I |   Layer 3
+---+---+---+                             +---+---+---+
   Layer 3       Layer 2   Layer 1```
[[H]] = 3, Toy cubes = ABCDEFGHIJKL ([[N]] = 12)
```============ Top View ============   ======== Front View ========
+---+---+---+
| A | B | C |   +---+---+                                 Layer 1
+---+---+---+   | J | K |                   +-+-+-+-+
| D | E | F |   +---+---+                   | L | K |     Layer 2
+---+---+---+   | L |                     +-+-+-+-+-+-+
| G | H | I |   +---+                     | G | H | I |   Layer 3
+---+---+---+                             +---+---+---+
   Layer 3       Layer 2   Layer 1```

data.stubGenerator:
read n:int h:int
read cubes:word(n)
write pyramid
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1>>: Two space-separated positive integers [[N]] and [[H]].
<<Line 2>>: [[N]] uppercase English letters ({{A}}-{{Z}}), denoting the labels of each toy cube (in order).
data.outputDescription: <<[[H]] lines>>: The front view of the constructed 3D pyramid. Each layer of the pyramid is represented as space-separated labels of the front-most toy cubes in that layer (if any), centred on the subsequent layer(s). Remove trailing whitespaces in each output line.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

There are N toy cubes of equal size, each labelled with some uppercase English letter (A-Z). Your job is to build a 3D pyramid using these cubes. The pyramid should have H layers; the layers are labelled 1 to H from top to bottom. However, you are told that you may not have enough toy cubes to construct all H layers.

From bottom to top, build the layer labelled i as follows:
• Mark out an ixi empty grid centred on top of the previously-built layers. The 3D pyramid is viewed such that the bottom-most row of the grid is the front-most row in the pyramid.
• Take the next toy cube (from the given order of cubes) and place it in the grid. The grid is filled row by row from top to bottom, and each row is filled from left to right.

The 3D pyramid will be fully built once all toy cubes are placed. How will the 3D pyramid look like from the front once it's been fully built?

Examples:
H = 3, Toy cubes = ABCDEFGHIJKLMN (N = 14)
============ Top View ============   ======== Front View ========
+---+---+---+                                 +---+
| A | B | C |   +---+---+                     | N |       Layer 1
+---+---+---+   | J | K |   +---+           +-+-+-+-+
| D | E | F |   +---+---+   | N |           | L | M |     Layer 2
+---+---+---+   | L | M |   +---+         +-+-+-+-+-+-+
| G | H | I |   +---+---+                 | G | H | I |   Layer 3
+---+---+---+                             +---+---+---+
   Layer 3       Layer 2   Layer 1

H = 3, Toy cubes = ABCDEFGHIJKL (N = 12)
============ Top View ============   ======== Front View ========
+---+---+---+
| A | B | C |   +---+---+                                 Layer 1
+---+---+---+   | J | K |                   +-+-+-+-+
| D | E | F |   +---+---+                   | L | K |     Layer 2
+---+---+---+   | L |                     +-+-+-+-+-+-+
| G | H | I |   +---+                     | G | H | I |   Layer 3
+---+---+---+                             +---+---+---+
   Layer 3       Layer 2   Layer 1





   

      
Input

      
Line 1: Two space-separated positive integers N and H.
Line 2: N uppercase English letters (A-Z), denoting the labels of each toy cube (in order).

   

   

      
Output

      
H lines: The front view of the constructed 3D pyramid. Each layer of the pyramid is represented as space-separated labels of the front-most toy cubes in that layer (if any), centred on the subsequent layer(s). Remove trailing whitespaces in each output line.

   

   

      
Constraints

      
1 ≤ H ≤ 10
1 ≤ N ≤ H(H+1)(2H+1)/6

   

   

      
Example

      

         

            
Input

            
14 3
ABCDEFGHIJKLMN

         

         

            
Output

            
  N
 L M
G H I
